name: aidge-export-arm-cortexm

metadata.name: aidge-export-arm-cortexm
metadata.summary: Aidge export for ARM CortexM systems

documentation:
# Aidge Export for ARM CortexM systems

This plugin is to add in your `Aidge` environment to create exports for ARM CortexM systems.

## Installation

### From Source

To install the export manager from the gitlab repository, 
run these commands in the Python environment where aidge is already installed.

```
git clone https://gitlab.eclipse.org/eclipse/aidge/aidge_export_arm_cortexm.git
cd aidge_export_arm_cortexm
pip install .
```

## License

Aidge has a Eclipse Public License 2.0, as found in the [LICENSE](LICENSE).

modules.name: aidge_export_arm_cortexm
documentation:
Aidge Export for ARM CortexM

Use this module to generate CPP exports for ARM CortexM boards.
This module has to be used with the Aidge suite

functions.name: aidge_export_arm_cortexm._CMSIS-NN.CMSIS-NN.Tests.UnitTest.unittest_targets.print_summary
documentation:
Return 0 if all test passed
Return 1 if all test completed but one or more failed
Return 2 if one or more tests did not complete or was not detected

functions.name: aidge_export_arm_cortexm._CMSIS-NN.CMSIS-NN.Tests.UnitTest.unittest_targets.test_targets
documentation:
Return 0 if successful
Return 3 if no targets are detected
Return 4 if no tests are found

functions.name: aidge_export_arm_cortexm._CMSIS-NN.CMSIS-NN.Tests.UnitTest.unittest_targets.parse_tests
documentation:
Generate test runners, extract and return path to unit test(s).
Also parse generated test runners to avoid warning: redundant redeclaration.
Return True if successful.

methods.name: aidge_export_arm_cortexm.operators.Scaling.__call__
documentation:
Get dictionnary of scale values in function of the mode
Possible modes:
- no_scaling
- floating_point
- fixed_point (16 or 32 bits)
- single_shift
- double_shift
